rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe but non-systemic steroid-modified tinea cruris — needs prompt prescription (topical + short oral antifungal) and skin-care advice with a secondary-infection safety-net; not an emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Hot/hard spreading swelling, thick foul-smelling pus, or fever
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the area becomes hot, hard and spreading, oozes thick smelly pus, or you get a fever — get urgent review.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Heavy labourer, sweats heavily, tight synthetic underwear
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise moisture control and clothing changes to prevent recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is jock itch made worse by the strong steroid cream. Stop that cream, use the antifungal cream plus the antifungal tablets, dry the groin with a separate towel, wear loose cotton underwear, and treat your athlete's foot. Watch for hot/pus/fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription; urgent care if secondary infection
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless secondary infection/fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document steroid-modified tinea cruris, topical + oral antifungal, drying/clothing and tinea-pedis treatment, and monitor for atrophy.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Tinea incognito spreads with worsening atrophy, or a secondary infection is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No antifungal or moisture/tinea-pedis advice, so it persists/relapses.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED referral for benign tinea cruris.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot KOH-scrape/Wood's-lamp remotely, but scrotal sparing + scaly advancing border is characteristic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as tinea cruris (stop steroid, topical + short oral antifungal, drying/tinea-pedis measures) and safety-net for infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Continuing steroids, or mistreating as candida/erythrasma.
